title: Post Fiat Producer Signal Schema
description: Canonical signal contract for the Post Fiat network. Defines the standard format for any producer emitting directional signals with calibrated confidence, regime context, and routing actions. Designed for machine consumption: downstream parsers, scoring bots, and intelligence layers (e.g. Hive Mind) can validate and ingest signals using only this schema and the companion validate_signal.py script.

semver.description: Semantic version string (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). MAJOR = breaking change to required fields, MINOR = new optional fields, PATCH = description/annotation fixes.

iso_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. MUST include timezone (UTC recommended). Signals without timezone are ambiguous and will fail validation.

direction.description: Predicted price direction over the horizon window. 'bullish' = price expected to rise, 'bearish' = price expected to fall. NOTE: during SYSTEMIC regime, bearish signals typically express capital preservation (low confidence), not high-conviction short recommendations. Consumers should check the 'action' field to determine whether to act.

action.description: Routing decision for this signal. EXECUTE = act on direction + confidence as stated. WITHHOLD = signal exists for record-keeping but should not be traded (e.g. regime suppression, insufficient confidence). INVERT = act opposite to stated direction (used for symbols where the producer's directional model is reliably wrong, e.g. anti-correlated). Consumers MUST respect this field: an INVERT signal with direction='bearish' means the consumer should treat it as bullish.

confidence.description: Signal confidence as a calibrated probability. 0.0 = no confidence, 1.0 = certainty. MUST be on 0-1 scale (not 0-100). A well-calibrated producer's 0.60 confidence should resolve correctly ~60% of the time. Consumers can verify calibration quality via the producer's proof surface.

regime_context.description: Market regime metadata. Provides context for WHY the signal has the confidence/action it does. Optional but strongly recommended for STANDARD and FULL conformance.

regime_id.description: Current regime classification. SYSTEMIC = correlated risk-off (signals suppressed, correlations spike). NEUTRAL = normal dispersion (signals most actionable). DIVERGENCE = sector rotation. EARNINGS = event-driven. UNKNOWN = insufficient data for classification.

regime_confidence.description: Confidence in the regime classification itself (0-1 scale). NOTE: some internal systems use 0-100 integer scale; this schema normalizes to 0-1. If your source uses 0-100, divide by 100 before emission.

proximity.description: Regime transition proximity score. 0.0 = deep in current regime (stable), 1.0 = transition imminent. Useful for consumers who want to adjust position sizing near regime boundaries.

duration_days.description: Calendar days since the current regime was entered. Combined with proximity, this enables duration-gated strategies (e.g. suppress signals in first N days of a new regime).

decision.description: Regime-level execution decision. NO_TRADE = capital preservation mode. EXECUTE = regime supports trading. MONITOR = watch for regime change. This is the regime-level version of the signal 'action' field.

calibration_metadata.description: How the producer derived confidence and direction. Enables downstream systems to assess signal quality without trusting a single number.

method.description: Calibration method identifier. Examples: 'empirical_conditional' (data-driven P(direction|regime,symbol)), 'model_ensemble' (multi-model voting), 'expert_judgment' (human-assigned).

sample_size.description: Number of historical observations used to derive this confidence level. Low sample sizes (< 30) should trigger consumer caution.

empirical_hit_rate.description: Observed accuracy on historical/backtest data for this (symbol, regime, horizon) combination. Consumers can compare this to the confidence field to check for overconfidence.

brier_score.description: Historical Brier score for this signal type. 0.25 = coin flip baseline for balanced outcomes. Lower is better. Enables consumers to filter on calibration quality.

weak_symbol_metadata.description: Diagnosis when a symbol's signal quality is below network standards. Present when the producer has identified this symbol as unreliable in its current direction.

weakness_score.description: Composite weakness score (0 = strong, 1 = maximally weak). Severity thresholds: NONE < 0.2, MILD < 0.4, MODERATE < 0.6, SEVERE >= 0.6.

severity.description: Human-readable severity bucket derived from weakness_score.

original_direction.description: The direction BEFORE inversion was applied. Only meaningful when action=INVERT. Allows consumers to see what the raw model predicted vs what the routing decision overrode.

inversion_p_value.description: Statistical significance of the inversion decision (z-test p-value). Low values (< 0.01) indicate strong evidence that inversion improves outcomes.

signal.title: Producer Signal
signal.description: The atomic unit of producer output on the Post Fiat network. Each signal represents a directional prediction for a specific symbol over a defined time horizon, with a calibrated confidence level and a routing action that tells consumers how to use it.

signal_id.description: Producer-assigned unique identifier. Recommended format: '{producer_prefix}-{SYMBOL}-{UNIX_TIMESTAMP}' (e.g. 'pf-BTC-1773960268'). Must be unique across all signals from this producer. Used for deduplication, traceability, and attribution window matching.

producer_id.description: Unique producer identifier as registered with the network or downstream consumer. This is the producer's public identity string, not a wallet address.

timestamp.description: Signal emission time. When this signal was generated and pushed. Must include timezone.

symbol.description: Ticker symbol in canonical short form. Use the base currency for crypto (BTC not BTC-USD or BTC/USD). Use standard exchange tickers for equities. All uppercase, alphanumeric only.

horizon_hours.description: Attribution window in hours. The time window over which the directional prediction should be evaluated. Common values: 1, 2, 4, 6, 8, 12, 24 (intraday), 168 (1 week), 336 (2 weeks). Consumers use this to know when to score the signal against realized outcomes.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema the signal conforms to. Consumers should check this to handle version-specific parsing.

attribution_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical signal payload (signal_id + symbol + direction + confidence + horizon_hours + timestamp, concatenated with '|' delimiter). Enables tamper detection and cross-surface traceability. Optional but required for FULL conformance.

metadata.description: Producer-specific extension fields. Anything not covered by the canonical schema goes here. Consumers should ignore fields they do not understand. Use this for internal tracking, model IDs, experiment tags, etc.

signal_batch.title: Signal Batch
signal_batch.description: A batch of signals emitted together (e.g. one push cycle). Useful for consumers ingesting multiple signals atomically.

regime_context.description: Batch-level regime context. If present, applies to all signals in the batch unless a signal has its own regime_context.

signals.description: Array of signals in this batch.

signal_count.description: Number of signals in this batch. Consumers can use this for integrity checks.
